hypothesis: If an LLM-based generative agent is augmented with external knowledge tools (memory, periodic table, knowledge base) and closed-loop feedback from diffusion-based structure generation and learned formation-energy evaluation, then it will achieve higher success rates within ±0.25 eV/atom of specified targets and higher V.U.N. scores than tool-free LLMs or diffusion-only baselines because tool-guided planning and iterative feedback efficiently steer exploration toward chemically plausible, novel regions of composition–structure space.
